ocr.text: In the intermodal transport volumes analysis for FedEx Corporation covering the years 2020, 2021, and 2022, the Total Volume
(TEUs) exhibited a growth from 150,000 in 2020 to 170,000 in 2021, reaching 200,000 in 2022, reflecting a Change of 30,000
TEUs and a % Change of 20%. The Rail Volume (TEUs) increased from 75,000 in 2020 to 90,000 in 2021 and further to
110,000 in 2022, with a Change of 20,000 TEUs and a % Change of 26.67%. The Road Volume (TEUs) rose from 50,000 in
2020 to 55,000 in 2021, and then to 60,000 in 2022, showing a Change of 5,000 TEUs and a % Change of 10%. The Sea
Volume (TEUs) remained stable at 25,000 in 2020 and 2021, increasing to 30,000 in 2022, resulting in a Change of 5,000 TEUs
and a % Change of 20%. Revenue increased from CHF 200M in 2020 to CHF 220M in 2021, and CHF 250M in 2022, with a
Change of CHF 30M and a % Change of 15%. Costs grew from CHF 150M in 2020 to CHF 160M in 2021, and CHF 180M in
2022, marking a Change of CHF 20M and a % Change of 13.33%. Profit rose from CHF 50M in 2020 to CHF 60M in 2021,
and CHF 70M in 2022 with a Change of CHF 10M and a % Change of 20%.
Year  IntermodalTransportVolumes
2020  2021  2022  Change  %Change
RoadVolume(TEUs)  50000  55000  60000  5000
Revenue(CHF)  200M  220M  250M  30M
Cost(CHF)  150M  160M  180M  20M
Profit(CHF)  50M  60M  70M  10M
MarketShare(%)  20%  22%  25%  3%
CustomerSatisfaction  85%  87%  90%  3%
On-TimeDelivery  92%  93%  95%  2%
Market Share increased from 20% in 2020 to 22% in 2021, reaching 25% in 2022, reflecting a Change of 3% and a % Change
of 15%. Customer Satisfaction improved from 85% in 2020 to 87% in 2021 and 90% in 2022, with a Change of 3% and a %
Change of 3.53%. Finally, On-Time Delivery rates rose from 92% in 2020 to 93% in 2021, and to 95% in 2022, indicating a
Change of 2% and a % Change of 2.17%.
